mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict returns during late-cycle phases or produces negative alpha when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally on inflation surprises or forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting consumer demand and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence between payroll growth and unemployment decline yields negative returns or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP revisions consistently fail to predict equity direction or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production shocks are priced in immediately with no subsequent 5-day drift or forward returns turn positive in more than 55% of cases.
